blurb:

blurb:

Jane is an adult, so she's past the unpredictable kitten phase and fully herself at this point. What you see is what you get, and based on her photo, what you get looks pretty great.

Her bio is still being built out by the rescue, so specific personality details aren't posted yet. But adult calicos with this kind of coloring and presence don't tend to blend into the background. blurb:

blurb:

blurb:

He's an adult male at Our Pals Place in Marietta, and his profile is still light on details â but Labs are one of those breeds where the basics tell you a lot.

Someone already put time into getting him through the wild puppy stage, and now he's at the age where he's got energy but can also settle down when the day is done. If you've got kids, a yard, or both, he's probably going to fit right in. If you've been thinking about a Lab but didn't want to start from scratch with a puppy, Junior might be exactly what you were waiting for.

His full story isn't posted yet, so the best next step is to contact Our Pals Place directly through Petfinder and ask about him. Go meet him in person â with Labs, it usually takes about four minutes before you're filling out paperwork.

blurb:

Maine Coon mixes tend to be curious, interactive, and a little more engaged with their people than your average cat. They want to know what you're doing. They want to be nearby.
